question.text: Options are known as 'wasting assets' because:

answer.text: Their time value gradually falls to zero by expiry date
They always result in a loss for the buyer
The underlying commodity deteriorates in quality
The exchange charges daily holding fees

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Options are called 'wasting assets' because the time value component of their premium decays to zero at expiry. Each passing day reduces the option's value, all else equal. **The Trap:** Options don't always result in losses. Physical commodity quality is irrelevant to option time decay. No daily holding fees are charged. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers time decay (theta) for equity options. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Time decay benefits option sellers — they earn premium that erodes daily. This gives sellers a structural advantage, which is why option selling strategies are popular among professional traders.
